grid-columns.$description: Column count for the layout grid. Web uses 12 for flexible content arrangements; mobile uses 4, giving halves and quarters where a single column is not enough.

grid-gutter.$description: Space between grid columns. Distinct from the spacing tokens — this is grid structure, not content rhythm.

max-content-width.$description: Outer container width — the widest anything goes. Reserved for imagery, thumbnails, and full-bleed sections that break out of the prose column.

max-text-width.$description: Prose column width — case study body text and article content. Holds line length at 65–75 characters no matter how wide the outer container gets. Never widen this for long-form reading.

small.$description: Tags and badges. Deliberately rectangular against an otherwise soft system — used rarely enough that the contrast reads as intent, not inconsistency.

medium.$description: Input fields and small components. The quiet default where a pill would be too much.

large.$description: Case study cards and content containers, roughly 720px wide. Scales with the element — a smaller radius would read as square at this size.

feature.$description: Large content surfaces — homepage case study thumbnails (~800px) and the article reader modal in the Write section. Scales with the element: at this width a smaller radius reads as square.

shell.$description: The main content container's bottom corners, where it scrolls up over the footer. The site's signature curve — sets the rounding vocabulary everything else answers to.

pill.$description: All buttons — primary and secondary. The shell's generous rounding makes fully-rounded buttons the consistent choice rather than an exception.

inline-gap.$description: Adjacent elements that read as one unit — kicker to title, icon to label, tag to tag. Same in both modes; tight relationships don't loosen on desktop.

component-padding.$description: Internal padding of small components — buttons, tags, input fields. Tighter than card-padding.

content-gap.$description: Elements inside a single thought — heading to its body text, thumbnail to its caption, fields in a form. Close enough that they still read as one unit.

card-padding.$description: Internal padding of cards and content containers — project tiles, case study cards. Pair with colour/surface/default.

block-gap.$description: Separate units inside one section — title group to image, text block to media. Distinct thoughts, same section.

page-margin.$description: Left and right page gutters. Mobile sits at the 16px platform minimum; web pairs with the max-width container.

section-gap.$description: Between major sections — problem to solution, work to about. The page's structural rhythm.

signature-gap.$description: Hero and key-section breathing room. A signature moment — two or three uses site-wide, never the default.

family.$description: Section headers and page titles. Uses the display face for signature weight — the only heading that does.

size.$description: Page titles and major section headers. The largest tokenised type. One per section, never stacked.

weight.$description: Bold only. Alte Haas Grotesk has no intermediate weights.

line-height.$description: 1.10 ratio — tight, so large type doesn't sprawl across the viewport.

letter-spacing.$description: Negative tracking tightens large type. The bigger the size, the more it needs.

family.$description: Subsection headers. Switches to the body face — calmer than heading-1, so hierarchy reads through size not face.

size.$description: Case study section headers and large card titles.

weight.$description: SemiBold — distinct from heading-1's bold without competing with it.

size.$description: Smallest heading. Breaks up long case study prose into scannable chunks.

weight.$description: SemiBold, matching heading-2 for a consistent heading voice.

size.$description: Case study intros, the opening 'here's the problem' paragraph, about-page lede. Sets up a section before body-medium takes over.

weight.$description: Regular. Weight is never used to emphasise body copy — size and color do that.

line-height.$description: 1.6 ratio — generous, for comfortable reading of longer passages.

letter-spacing.$description: No tracking adjustment at reading sizes.

size.$description: The bulk of case study prose. Same in both modes — 16px is the readability floor on desktop and the comfortable default on mobile.

line-height.$description: 1.6 ratio. Cap line length at 65–75 characters for long-form reading.

letter-spacing.$description: No tracking adjustment at reading sizes.

size.$description: Sidebar notes, in-prose image captions, compact secondary content. At the mobile minimum — never goes smaller.

letter-spacing.$description: No tracking adjustment at reading sizes.

size.$description: Button text, form labels, navigation items. 16px suits a content-first site where each button is a moment, not a dense toolbar control.

weight.$description: Medium. Distinguishes interactive text from body copy at the same size.

line-height.$description: 1.4 ratio — tight, since labels are single-line.

letter-spacing.$description: Slight positive tracking improves legibility in buttons and separates label from body-medium.

size.$description: Tags on case study thumbnails, badges, chips. Non-interactive — no touch-target requirement. Never for anything read at length.

weight.$description: Medium, matching label. Keeps the UI-text family visually consistent.

line-height.$description: 1.33 ratio — tight, since these always sit single-line inside a pill.

letter-spacing.$description: More open than label — small text at medium weight needs the air, and it separates tags from caption at the same size.

family.$description: Annotation face. Mono signals metadata, not code.

size.$description: Timestamps, image credits, project role/date lines. Pair with colour/text/tertiary. 12px is the floor — never smaller.

letter-spacing.$description: Mono is already wide — no tracking needed.
